results.reason: A river down the west edge plus a continuous dense tree-line on the north and east bound the village; it is clearly not open field.
results.title: Bounded perimeter

results.reason: Stone paths wind and branch at irregular angles around the buildings and farm, with varied width and no single straight spine.
results.title: Organic branching paths

results.reason: The narrow bridge compresses movement then releases into the statue plaza, and paths meet each building at its door.
results.title: Path rhythm and door alignment

results.reason: Six-plus buildings of visibly different massing and roof pitch, several sharing walls into clusters rather than one repeated shell.
results.title: Varied building massing

results.reason: A coherent warm-timber building set reads against grass, stone path, and water as one place, distinct from the ground.
results.title: Distinct material identity

results.reason: Barrels, crates, a fenced crop plot, lily pads, stumps and ivy scatter through the interior and cluster at path edges and water.
results.title: Decoration density

results.reason: The pedestal statue at the central crossroads is the unambiguous first-read focal point.
results.title: Dominant landmark

results.reason: It instantly reads as a riverside farming village with a clear place to move toward.
results.title: Readable first view

results.reason: Barrels and crates are grouped and dressed beside buildings, not lone unstyled markers.
results.title: Dressed reward clusters

results.reason: The river-and-bridge crossing plus a lily-pad pond act as a water landmark and organic plaza.
results.title: Water or organic plaza

results.reason: A small reused material set (grass, dirt, stone, water, timber) runs throughout with no unrelated materials.
results.title: Limited coherent vocabulary

results.reason: 'The farming village with the river, the bridge, and the statue' is immediately statable.
results.title: Memorable identity
